name: Disease & Drug
guideline:
Disease & Drug Annotation Guideline

Overview

Annotate clinical text to identify diseases/conditions and drugs/medications, assign normalization codes, and link treatments to the conditions they address.

Entity Types

disease

Mark any mention of a disease, disorder, symptom, or clinical finding (e.g., type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, headache). Annotate the minimal span that fully identifies the condition.

drug

Mark any medication, drug name, or pharmacological agent (e.g., metformin, lisinopril). Include dosage only when it is part of the named entity span recognized in the source vocabulary.

Attributes


Relation: treatment

Draw a treatment relation from a drug entity to the disease entity it treats when the therapeutic relationship is explicitly stated in the text (e.g., “atorvastatin for hyperlipidemia”).

Key Rules

  1. Annotate all mentions, including historical and family history references.
  2. Apply certainty based on the author’s assertion, not the annotator’s clinical knowledge.
  3. Do not annotate generic drug classes (e.g., “antibiotics”) unless a specific agent is named.
  4. When the same span could be both a disease and a symptom, prefer the most specific entity type supported by context.
